title: cordylanthus maritimus subsp. canescens
intro: Cordylanthus maritimus subsp. canescens, also known as Salt Marsh Bird's-beak, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Orobanchaceae family, which is a type of parasitic flowering plant. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and prevent infection. The health benefits of Salt Marsh Bird's-beak include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, such as nausea and stomach pain, as well as its ability to act as an expectorant to relieve respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of Salt Marsh Bird's-beak can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional folk medicine, but it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using the plant for medicinal purposes.
